text: Many experts believe we are moving toward general AI, or AGI – a type of artificial intelligence that can perform any intellectual task a human can. Unlike today's narrow AI, which excels only at specific jobs, AGI would think, learn, and adapt across many fields. The potential benefits are huge: faster medical diagnoses, solving climate problems, and personalized education. However, risks also exist, including job displacement and ethical concerns about control. Predictions for when AGI will arrive vary widely; some say within ten years, while others think it could take decades. One thing is clear: the future of AI will profoundly shape our world.
